title: UK elderly daily health check-in with fall-risk early detection (frontend)

requirements.summary: Onboarding flow for 65+ users to capture and persist a minimal fall-risk context profile per authenticated Internet Identity user.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can sign in with Internet Identity and complete onboarding.
Age < 65 is blocked with a clear English message explaining the app is for adults 65+.
Profile data is saved to the Motoko backend and is available on subsequent sessions.

file_operations.description: Create the app shell with authenticated gating (Internet Identity), onboarding routing/flow, and post-onboarding navigation to daily check-in and history. Use the authorization component patterns (Internet Identity login/logout, clearing React Query cache on logout) to ensure profile data is only shown when authenticated. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Implement a login/logout button using the authorization component guidance (useInternetIdentity + React Query cache clear on logout), and expose it for use in the header. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create the onboarding UI for older adults to collect profile fields (age 65+ required, optional sex/height/weight, mobility aids, falls last 12 months yes/no + count, conditions checkboxes, balance-affecting medications yes/no) with simple language and large touch targets; block age < 65 with a clear message; submit profile via backend actor and handle friendly errors. Use shadcn-ui form primitives (e.g., Card, Button, Input, Checkbox/Switch, Alert) for consistent accessible UI. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add React Query hooks to load/save the caller profile (getCallerUserProfile/saveCallerUserProfile) and provide derived onboarding state (needsOnboarding) that avoids UI flashing while the actor initializes (per authorization guidance). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a reusable layout with a simple header (app name + LoginButton) and footer area for disclaimers/links; ensure all primary screens can reuse consistent spacing and typography. Use shadcn-ui layout primitives (e.g., Card, Separator) for consistent styling. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Daily check-in form with one submission per UK local calendar day; prevent duplicates and allow viewing the submitted answers for the day.
acceptanceCriteria: A signed-in user can submit a check-in and see a confirmation screen.
The app prevents a second submission for the same UK date (with an option to view the submitted answers).
Check-ins are stored and retrievable from the backend for the signed-in user.

file_operations.description: Create the daily check-in form UI (dizziness, unsteadiness, new pain, fatigue/weakness, vision issues, medication changes today, alcohol intake, sleep quality, near-fall/fall) with large controls and plain-English labels; compute today's UK local date key; pre-check whether today's submission already exists and, if so, block re-submission and show a 'View today's answers' action. Use shadcn-ui components (Card, Button, Checkbox/Switch, Alert) for accessible form UI. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a confirmation screen shown after successful submission with clear next steps (view risk results, view history). Use shadcn-ui components (Card, Button, Alert) for consistent styling. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a read-only detail view for a specific UK date that displays saved check-in answers (used for 'view submitted answers' when the day is already submitted and for history drill-down). Use shadcn-ui components for readable, high-contrast summaries. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add React Query hooks/mutations for: getCheckInByDate(todayUkDate), submitCheckIn, and getCallerCheckIns(limit) with cache keys scoped to the authenticated caller; ensure invalidation/refetch after submission. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Implement a small utility to compute a stable UK local date string (Europe/London) for the 'one submission per calendar day' rule and consistent display formatting.
Wire the daily check-in, confirmation, and check-in detail views into the app navigation/routing, ensuring they are only accessible to authenticated users who have completed onboarding.

requirements.summary: Display the deterministic, rules-based fall-risk assessment (score, level, contributing factors) for the latest check-in and keep it stable across reloads.
acceptanceCriteria: After submitting a check-in, the user sees risk score + level + contributing factors.
The backend exposes an API that returns the computed risk result for a given check-in.
Risk results for the same inputs are stable across reloads (no randomness).

file_operations.description: Create the risk results UI that shows numeric score (0–100), risk level (Low/Medium/High), and plain-English contributing factors returned by the backend; ensure the screen is readable for older adults and includes appropriate non-alarming presentation. Use shadcn-ui components (Card, Badge/Alert, Button) for consistent results styling. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add React Query hooks to fetch the latest computed risk assessment (getCurrentRiskAssessment) and to support history detail rendering (e.g., using getRiskHistory with the caller principal) with proper loading/error states. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a reusable, high-contrast summary card for risk score/level/factors to reuse on results and history detail screens. Use shadcn-ui primitives for consistent visuals. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Wire the risk results screen into navigation/routing as the post-check-in next step and allow access from history detail when risk data is available.

requirements.summary: Provide tailored, non-alarming UK guidance by risk level with a persistent medical disclaimer (no diagnosis, no emergency response).
acceptanceCriteria: Low/Medium/High risk screens each show guidance appropriate to the level.
UI contains a persistent disclaimer (e.g., in results and settings/about).
Copy references UK-appropriate services (GP/NHS 111) without initiating calls/messages.

file_operations.description: Add English-only, UK-focused copy blocks for Low/Medium/High guidance and a standard disclaimer (not a medical device, no diagnosis, no emergency response; references GP/NHS 111 without initiating calls/messages).

Create a reusable disclaimer component for persistent display (e.g., in results, settings/about, and optionally footer). Use shadcn-ui Alert for readable, non-alarming prominence. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Render tailored guidance based on risk level using shared copy, and include the persistent disclaimer on the results screen. Use shadcn-ui components for consistent information hierarchy. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add a small persistent area (e.g., footer) that can show a compact disclaimer and links to About/How it works/Privacy so users can always find limitations and policy information.

requirements.summary: History view to review past check-ins and risk levels over time with accessible, high-contrast summaries and drill-down per day; data loaded via React Query.
acceptanceCriteria: User can view a list of previous check-ins with date and risk level.
Selecting an item shows the saved answers and computed risk details for that day.
Data is loaded via React Query with proper loading and error states.

file_operations.description: Create a history screen showing the last ~30 entries as an accessible high-contrast list (and optionally a simple chart-like visual using bars/markers) with date + risk level; selecting an item navigates to the day detail. Use shadcn-ui components (Card, Button, Tabs/Accordion if needed) for consistent accessible UI. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a history detail screen that shows the saved answers for that day plus computed risk details (reusing RiskSummaryCard) with clear headings and large typography. Use shadcn-ui components for consistent structure. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add React Query hook(s) that load history using backend functions that return check-ins and associated risk (prefer getRiskHistory with the authenticated caller principal), including loading skeletons and friendly error states with retry. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a consistent loading UI for primary screens (large, readable, non-jarring). Use shadcn-ui Skeleton or equivalent primitives if available. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a consistent error UI with plain-English messaging and a retry action that can be reused across onboarding, check-in, results, and history. Use shadcn-ui Alert + Button for consistency. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Wire History and History Detail pages into navigation/routing and ensure they are protected behind authentication and completed onboarding.

requirements.summary: Older-adult-friendly UI with large type, high contrast, clear touch targets; add accessibility settings for text size and contrast mode persisted locally.
acceptanceCriteria: Primary screens meet a “large type” layout without clipping or overlapping.
Users can change text size and contrast mode and see the change immediately.
Settings persist across reloads (local storage is acceptable).

file_operations.description: Implement a local-settings hook that persists text size (normal/large/largest) and contrast mode (on/off) to localStorage and applies settings immediately (e.g., toggling CSS classes on the document root).

Create an accessibility settings panel UI with text size choices and contrast toggle, designed for older adults (large controls, clear labels). Use shadcn-ui components (Switch/RadioGroup/Button, Card) to ensure consistent touch targets and accessibility. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add global styles and CSS variables/classes to support large default typography, increased line-height, high contrast mode, and safe spacing/touch targets; ensure styles apply across onboarding, check-in, results, history, and info pages (and avoid blue/purple primary palette).
Add navigation access to Accessibility Settings and ensure layout spacing supports large text without clipping/overlap.
Wire the accessibility settings page into navigation/routing and apply the accessibility settings provider/hook at the app root so changes reflect immediately across all screens.

requirements.summary: Coherent visual theme suitable for a UK health check-in product; apply consistently across screens and avoid a blue/purple primary palette.
acceptanceCriteria: App has a consistent theme applied across all pages.
Theme avoids blue & purple as the primary palette.
Components are visually consistent (buttons, cards, headings, alerts).

file_operations.description: Define theme tokens (CSS variables) for background/foreground, primary/secondary/accent, borders, and focus states using a non-blue/non-purple primary palette (e.g., green/teal with warm neutral accents), and ensure high-contrast readability.
Adjust Tailwind theme extension (if needed) to align with the new CSS variable-based theme and ensure typography/spacing defaults support older-adult readability (e.g., comfortable base font sizing).

Create a single reusable primary action button wrapper (large touch target, consistent sizing) to be used across onboarding/check-in/results/history to keep UI consistent. Use shadcn-ui Button as the base component. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Adopt shared themed components (cards, buttons, alerts) and ensure headings/spacing match the global theme consistently. Use shadcn-ui components consistently. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Adopt shared themed components and ensure consistent visual hierarchy and touch target sizing per the app theme. Use shadcn-ui components consistently. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Apply consistent themed risk-level styling (non-alarming color usage, high contrast) via shared components and theme tokens. Use shadcn-ui components consistently. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Apply consistent themed list/summary styling and ensure readability for older adults using shared components and theme tokens. Use shadcn-ui components consistently. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Client-side validation and friendly error handling for forms and backend/network failures with actionable messages and retry options.
acceptanceCriteria: Invalid or incomplete forms cannot be submitted and show inline messages.
Backend rejects duplicate same-day submissions and frontend shows an actionable message.
Network/backend failures show a retry option and do not blank the UI.

file_operations.description: Add utilities to normalize backend/network errors into plain-English messages (e.g., authorization required, duplicate same-day check-in, validation issues) without exposing raw exceptions.

Add client-side validation (age required and >= 65; fall count required when falls=yes; numeric bounds for optional height/weight when present) and show inline friendly messages; handle backend errors using the shared error utilities and ErrorState component. Use shadcn-ui form feedback patterns (Alert, field messages) for consistent UI. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add required-field validation for daily check-in (ensure all required toggles/questions are answered before submit), and handle duplicate-submission rejections and transient failures with plain-English messaging and a retry option; avoid blank screens by using LoadingState/ErrorState components. Use shadcn-ui components for consistent feedback UI. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Ensure React Query errors render via ErrorState with retry and do not blank the UI; include clear messaging for unauthenticated/unauthorized access (prompt to sign in). Use shadcn-ui Alert/Button for consistency. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add a consistent unauthenticated state (sign-in prompt) and ensure logout clears cached queries (per authorization guidance) so personal data is not shown after logout. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add static informational pages (About, How it works, Privacy) in English, UK-focused, and accessible from navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Pages are accessible from navigation.
Copy explains data handling and limitations clearly in English.
No references to unsupported integrations (SMS, push notifications, external analytics).

file_operations.description: Create an About page with UK-focused explanation of the app purpose, limitations, and disclaimer in English (no emergency response, no diagnosis). Use shadcn-ui typography/card components for readable layout. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a 'How it works (rules-based risk)' page explaining the transparent, rules-based nature of the risk assessment in plain English, without claiming certainty. Use shadcn-ui components for readable structure. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a Privacy page explaining what data is stored in the canister, that no third-party services are used, and that the app is UK-focused; keep copy in English and avoid unsupported integrations. Use shadcn-ui components for readable structure. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add navigation links to About/How it works/Privacy (and ensure they remain accessible and readable with large text and high contrast).
Wire About/How it works/Privacy pages into routing and ensure they are reachable from navigation; keep them accessible without implying any external integrations.

requirements.summary: Add generated branding/usability images as static assets and render them in the UI without backend involvement.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated images exist in frontend/public/assets/generated and are referenced in the React UI.
Images load correctly in production build without backend involvement.

file_operations.description: Add the generated logo image asset as a static file in the required generated assets directory so it can be referenced by the React UI.
Add the generated hero illustration asset as a static file in the required generated assets directory so it can be referenced by the React UI.
Add the generated monochrome icon set asset as a static file in the required generated assets directory so it can be referenced by the React UI.

Create a small branding header component that renders the generated logo (frontend/public/assets/generated/uk-fallcheck-logo.dim_512x512.png) and app name with high-contrast, large-type styling. Use shadcn-ui layout primitives if helpful. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Render the generated hero illustration (frontend/public/assets/generated/checkin-hero.dim_1600x900.png) on the onboarding/intro area to improve usability and reassurance; ensure it remains accessible (alt text in English) and does not add extra steps.

Render relevant parts of the generated icon set (frontend/public/assets/generated/risk-icons-set.dim_1024x1024.png) near corresponding questions (e.g., dizzy/unsteady/sleep) to aid comprehension; ensure icons are decorative or have appropriate English alt text.
Use the BrandHeader in the app header so branding is consistently visible across core screens.
